page: Google Cloud Platform n4a-highmem-48 is an x86_64 architecture server instance belonging to the n4a family. It features 48 dedicated virtual CPUs and 384 GB of system memory, yielding a memory-to-vCPU ratio of 8 GB per core. This instance does not include local storage or integrated GPU hardware, and it is provisioned with zero complimentary public IPv4 addresses.

The high memory density of 8 GB per vCPU positions this instance for memory-intensive workloads that require substantial RAM without requiring proportional increases in compute cores. This resource allocation profile offers qualitative cost efficiency by allowing organizations to scale memory capacity independently of excessive CPU licensing or provisioning costs.

Due to its memory-optimized configuration, the n4a-highmem-48 is suited for memory-intensive applications, large-scale databases, in-memory caching layers, and enterprise workloads that demand significant memory footprints but do not require local NVMe storage or GPU acceleration.
description: Google Cloud Platform n4a-highmem-48 is an x86_64 architecture server instance within the n4a family, configured with 48 dedicated vCPUs and 384 GB of RAM. This configuration provides a high memory-to-vCPU ratio of 8 GB per core, making it a memory-optimized resource. The instance does not feature local storage or integrated GPUs, and it includes zero complimentary public IPv4 addresses. Qualitatively, the high memory density allows for cost-efficient scaling of memory-bound applications without over-provisioning compute resources. The n4a-highmem-48 is suited for workloads such as in-memory databases, caching systems, and enterprise applications requiring a large memory footprint.

tagline: A memory-optimized x86_64 server configuration featuring forty-eight dedicated virtual cores and three hundred eighty-four gigabytes of system memory.
